2605.24183.title: AvalancheBench: Evaluating Enterprise Data Agents Through Latent World Recovery
2605.24183.paper_summary: We introduce AvalancheBench, a benchmark for evaluating enterprise data agents through \emph{latent world recovery}. AvalancheBench improves on existing benchmarks in three ways. First, it evaluates analytical understanding rather than pipeline completion: systems are scored on whether they recover the segments, drivers, temporal events, and relationships that explain the data, not merely on whether they execute a workflow or produce a plausible report. Second, it provides ground truth for goal-driven analytics by generating observations from a known latent world, enabling partial credit for incomplete but valid recoveries. Third, it exposes how early analytical mistakes propagate into later conclusions: missed segments, merged events, or wrong attributions can lead to systematically wrong recommendations. In this sense, AvalancheBench complements real-data benchmarks by providing a controlled setting for diagnosing whether agents recover the analytical structure behind enterprise data. On a first e-commerce use case, the strongest configuration of a leading coding agent recovers only 26\% of the rubric, with failures concentrated in generic customer segmentations and merged temporal events.
2605.24183.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了 AvalancheBench，一個通過 \emph{潛在世界恢復} 來評估企業數據代理的基準測試。AvalancheBench 在三個方面改進了現有的基準測試。首先，它評估分析理解而非管道完成：系統的評分基於它們是否恢復了解釋數據的片段、驅動因素、時間事件和關係，而不僅僅是基於它們是否執行了一個工作流程或生成了一個合理的報告。第二，它通過從已知的潛在世界生成觀察，為目標驅動的分析提供了真實數據，從而使不完整但有效的恢復能夠獲得部分分數。第三，它揭示了早期分析錯誤如何傳播到後來的結論：錯過的片段、合併的事件或錯誤的歸因可能導致系統性錯誤的建議。在這個意義上，AvalancheBench 通過提供一個受控環境來診斷代理是否恢復了企業數據背後的分析結構，從而補充了真實數據基準測試。在第一個電子商務用例中，領先編碼代理的最強配置僅恢復了 26\% 的評分標準，失敗主要集中在通用客戶細分和合併的時間事件上。
2605.24183.author: Darek Kleczek et.al.
2605.24183.authors: Darek Kleczek, Fuheng Zhao, Alexander W. Lee, Julien Tissier, Pawel Liskowski, Ugur Cetintemel, Anupam Datta
